title: Travel time measures for the Strategic Road Network and local ‘A’ roads: January to December 2024
body: " Our statistical practice is regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing congestion statistics with any comments about how we meet these standards.   On the Strategic Road Network (SRN) for the year ending December 2024, the average delay is estimated to be 11.7 seconds per vehicle per mile (spvpm), compared to free flow, an 11.4% increase on year ending December 2023.  The average speed is estimated to be 55.9 mph for the year ending December 2024, down 1.9% from December 2023.  On local ‘A’ roads for the year ending December 2024, the average delay is estimated to be 48.3 seconds per vehicle per mile (spvpm) compared to free flow. This is up 3.4% on year ending December 2023.  The average speed is estimated to be 23.0 mph for the year ending December 2024, down 1.7% on year ending December 2023.  Contact us  Road congestion and travel times     Email congestion.stats@dft.gov.uk     Media enquiries 0300 7777 878       To hear more about DfT statistical publications as they are released, follow us on X at DfTstats.  "
